pattern: Because a number and its reverse must each lie below the target, you only have to test candidates up to the target itself and check digit-reversed sums.
hints: If num equals a plus reverse(a), how large can a possibly be relative to num?
Every candidate a is bounded by num, so a single linear sweep over candidates is enough.
For each a from 0 to num, compute a plus the integer made by reversing a's decimal digits; if any sum equals num, the answer is true.

edge_inputs.description: zero is expressible as 0 plus reverse(0)

stress.description: random values across the full allowed range to stress the candidate sweep
